validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Historical evolution is meaningful after understanding what an OS is.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: OS families are categorized after understanding what an OS is.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Hardware basics are foundational for understanding OS internals.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Core OS concepts extend the basic definition of what an OS does.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Concepts like interrupts and privilege modes depend on hardware basics.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: System calls are the programmatic interface to OS abstractions.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Kernel structure (monolithic, micro, hybrid) is clearer after the system-call interface.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Virtualization relies on hardware-level abstractions like privilege rings.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Hypervisor vs OS layering relates to system organization concepts.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Processes are the central OS abstraction for execution.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Threads are lightweight execution units within a process.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: IPC mechanisms facilitate communication between separate processes.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Shared-memory IPC and thread synchronization are closely related concepts.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Synchronization primitives exist to coordinate concurrent threads.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Shared resources in IPC require synchronization to avoid race conditions.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Scheduling policies govern which process or thread gets CPU time.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Modern schedulers operate at thread granularity.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Deadlock arises from misuse of synchronization primitives (locks, semaphores).

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Multi-threaded resource contention is the primary deadlock scenario.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Deadlocks are modeled over process resource dependency graphs.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Scheduling and resource allocation policies affect deadlock likelihood.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Memory management starts from physical memory hardware behavior.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Address spaces and allocation are core OS abstraction concepts.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Virtual memory extends baseline physical memory management with paging.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Per-process address spaces tie virtual memory to the process model.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: I/O subsystems depend on device-level hardware concepts (DMA, interrupts).

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: I/O operations are exposed to user space via system calls.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: File systems are built over block I/O abstractions.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Buffer cache and page cache interactions connect file systems and memory.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Protection model builds on fundamental OS abstractions like privilege levels.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: Access control is enforced at system-call boundaries.

validated_edges.subject: os
validated_edges.rationale: File permissions and ACLs are key protection topics in file systems.
